api: Zendesk Sunshine legacy custom objects API (https://developer.zendesk.com/api-reference/custom-data/custom-objects-api/custom-objects-api/)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion over the legacy (pre-2026 'Sunshine') custom objects API, which Zendesk is sunsetting July 27, 2026: every practical GET list resource is a stream, and every mutation still genuinely supported today (Zendesk blocked NEW object/relationship TYPE creation as of January 15, 2026, but record-level create/update/delete of EXISTING types remains supported) is a writes.json action. Detail-by-id endpoints are excluded as duplicate_of their list stream; type/relationship-type creation is excluded as deprecated (blocked by Zendesk itself, not a dialect limitation); search, jobs, and events are excluded as out_of_scope for the reasons noted per-endpoint.

excluded.reason: detail-by-key view of a record already fully emitted by the object_types list stream

Zendesk blocked creating new legacy object types as of January 15, 2026 (documented sunset ahead of the full legacy API removal on July 27, 2026); the endpoint still exists but every request against it now fails account-side

updates a legacy object type's schema; Zendesk's own docs describe legacy object types as immutable in practice once created and the platform is being sunset -- not modeled as a live write given the imminent removal

the Search Legacy Object Records endpoint takes a nested comparison-operator query DSL (property + operator/value pairs, optional _created_at/_updated_at range objects) that has no declarative representation in this dialect's flat query-param/body-field model; the objects stream's own config-driven ?type= filter (see spec.json's object_type) covers the common single-type-filter case
Legacy Custom Objects Events is itself deprecated per Zendesk's own docs and was limited to accounts enrolled in a now-closed EAP; not available to new/most accounts
detail-by-key view of a record already fully emitted by the relationship_types list stream
Zendesk blocked creating new legacy relationship types as of January 15, 2026

returns the set of object records related to one specific record via one specific relationship type -- the same relationship data the relationships stream's ?type= filter already exposes, scoped further by a single source record id the declarative dialect has no per-record fan-out-by-path-segment primitive for without a fan_out spec keyed on an id list this bundle does not otherwise collect

asynchronous bulk-batch job submission (fire-and-forget POST returning a job id, requiring separate polling); this dialect's write model is synchronous per-record and has no job-polling follow-up primitive
